entry.title: Modeling in SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management
entry.content:
Sales planners are always looking for confirmation that their compensation strategies are in line with their business goals. They want to make sure that their plans motivate sales teams at all levels and that they understand how any adjustments will affect the entire organization. Additionally, they need to consider the financial implications of implementing new plans. 

 

SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management's modeling feature offers upgraded capabilities that provide administrators with the tools they need to make informed decisions and optimize compensation plans effectively.

 

How does modeling work?

 

SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management offers an easy-to-use modeling feature that helps administrators test out different compensation plan scenarios before implementing them. This helps ensure that any proposed changes align with the company's goals and yield the desired outcomes.

 

The modeling tool provides a detailed analysis at both the macro and micro-levels. It allows administrators to see the big-picture impact, as well as the individual payee-level effects. By using data from the past, the tool can predict the outcomes of proposed changes accurately.

 

Navigating the Modeling Workspace

The Modeling workspace serves as a dedicated sandbox environment, distinct from production data. This demarcation ensures that changes made during the modeling process remain isolated until finalized. New objects created for modeling purposes are shielded from visibility in the production environment, preserving data integrity.

 

Accessing the Modeling application requires appropriate permission settings configured within the Incentive Management Admin interface. Administrators must navigate through Manage Setup > Security > Roles > Permissions to manage access effectively.

 

 

Clear delineation between modeling and production environments mitigates the risk of unintended consequences, fostering a controlled and secure modeling environment.

Moreover, the Modeling application is yet another fully integrated component of the Horizon theme, ensuring a seamless user experience across the SAP SuccessFactors suite.

 

 

 

 

Creating and Running Models

Creating a new model is a straightforward process within the Modeling application. After selecting "Create New Model" from the dropdown menu, administrators provide essential details such as the model's name, description, and relevant parameters. These parameters include the calendar for source/planning periods and the source date for model data extraction.

 

 

 

Once the model is configured, administrators can review the summary before initiating a model run. This step triggers the generation of model period data based on historical primary measurements and specified parameters.
Administrators are immediately notified after the model run is completed, with the option to view detailed reports for further analysis.

 


Introducing  Notifications

In line with the user-centric approach of SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management, we are introducing two key enhancement: Notifications. Like the My Incentive Dashboard feature, these updates prioritize user experience and streamline administrative tasks.

These enhancement underscore our commitment to provide users with intuitive tools that simplify complex tasks and foster collaboration across the organization. With Notifications, SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management continues to set the standard for user-centric innovation in compensation management.

 

 

 

Further Exploration

For a comprehensive understanding of advanced modeling options, administrators can refer to additional documentation provided by SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management  

 

SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management's Modeling feature equips administrators to make data-driven decisions with confidence. By providing a structured framework for scenario planning and analysis, the tool enables organizations to quickly adjust to changing market dynamics and drive sustainable growth

 

With the ability to model, simulate, and analyze compensation plans comprehensively, organizations can navigate the complexities of incentive management with precision and foresight, unlocking strategic insights for continued success.

 

 It's worth noting that each component of this feature is delivered in a phased approach, ensuring incremental value delivery and user satisfaction.

entry.title: Is It Time To Spring Clean Your Business With Cloud ERP?
entry.content:
By Zoryana Zagorodnya, SAP

Spring is in the air, and it’s the perfect time of the year for companies of all shapes and sizes to dust off the cobwebs, re-think their choices and transform how they do business. Keeping up with the latest rules, regulations and innovations—and having the right data to respond to everyday disruptions —is critical.

A “clean core” approach to your ERP system is a good place to start as the business can more easily consume the latest software releases, accurate data and the latest innovations in AI and sustainability. A clean core powered by Cloud ERP, also enables greater transparency and informed decision-making.

‘‘Cloud ERP and its advanced features and flexibility make it an essential ingredient for success in the digital age, where customers expect faster delivery of products and services, nonstop product and service improvements, greater reliability, and lower costs,’’ said Eric van Rossum, Chief Marketing Officer Cloud ERP at SAP.

New season, new technologies

Many companies are seeing AI is a game changer to leverage the vast amounts of data now available both within business systems and across the supply chain.

According to a recent study conducted by Forbes Advisor, a significant number of business owners, who currently utilize or intend to integrate AI into their operations reported that 53% employ AI to enhance production processes, while 51% embrace AI for process automation.

Embedding AI into end-to-end business processes allows companies to utilize the vast amount of data accessible through modern ERP systems.

“AI is a game changer for ERP,” said Kelly Amaroso, Senior Director, Cloud ERP Product Marketing at SAP in a recent Future of ERP podcast. “It's going to allow you to supercharge your business. AI that's integrated into your ERP, so that you can unlock incredible efficiency, gain extremely valuable insights, and a seamless, simple user experience that will allow for unmatched productivity and growth.”

Clearly, companies need to put a “spring in their step” to meet growing customer expectations. This requires the ability to calculate optimal stock levels to meet the service level commitments.

‘‘AI will be able to do calculations and provide insights into the optimal stock level, the reorder point, and the inventory allocation, based on predefined rules and industry's best practices,” said Carlos Portillo, Artificial Intelligence Product Marketing Mgr., Cloud ERP at SAP in the Future of Supply Chain podcast. ‘‘It can help reduce inventory levels across the supply chain, reducing stock outs and lost sales.”

Technological advancements are always happening, but with the introduction of generative AI, the pace seems to have accelerated to achieve these business goals and respond to the drivers that are out there.

 

To learn more about the ERP clean core strategy, and how to leverage AI in your business system, listen to a recent Rise into the Future webinar to hear from executives from SAP, McKinsey and Lexmark.

entry.title: Re: Last Indicator Value in Line Monitor POD within SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Hi @raquel_fernandes,

great to see you love the Last Indicator Value plugins !

Regarding your features requested I kindly would like to ask if you could raise those as a feature request through the SAP Innovation Management tool ?  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3537

I can already tease you that some of the requested features we are already working on 😉

Regarding the question of storing the values: At moment they are not stored, we are working on a feature that stores the last 5 values in a Cache in the backend.

Thanks you very much,

Marcel Wolf

SAP Digital Manufacturing Product Management

entry.title: How to see the package number in sap pm maintenance order list?
entry.content:
I have created a maintenance strategy time based with the packages like daily, weekly, monthly, half yearly, yearly.

Created an equipment task list & mentioned the operations.

Those operations are linked to their respective package number.

A strategy plan is created. The task list is assigned. Then the plan is scheduled.

Now the maintenance orders are getting generated.

There are few orders triggered as i can see through iw38.

The generated order contains a few operations (that is not all operations which i mentioned in the task list. And i know that this is due to the package assignment) but how can I know that which package operations are being shown.

How to see the package number in the maintenance orders list?Where can I view the package numbers other than going into task list?
